title: What Does Good Enough with My Body Look Like?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for releasing all-or-nothing body thinking
excerpt: A set of six journal prompts for when the body relationship is stuck in all-or-nothing. The prompts move from a broad orienting question toward a specific picture of what a workable relationship with the body actually looks like for you.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 9 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the body relationship feels like a binary: either you're doing everything right or you've failed entirely.

content.text: A journal prompt set for the all-or-nothing body relationship. The binary of "doing everything" or "having blown it" leaves no room for a middle that most people can actually live in.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
journal prompts as practice
first.
The practice
Warm-up
Where is the all-or-nothing in my body relationship right now?
Write the specific version of it. Not "I'm all-or-nothing" in general but the thing that split last week, or this morning.
What does "perfect" look like in this scenario?
Write out the standard you're holding yourself to. Get it on paper so you can look at it.
Closer in
What does "good enough" with my body actually look like?
Not ideal. Not minimal. The version that is liveable, repeatable, and yours.
Where in my body relationship am I already at "good enough"?
Name one specific thing you do consistently that counts.
Who taught me that partial counts as nothing?
Where did the binary come from? A parent, a diet, a decade?
What would change if some-of-the-time counted?
Write what your week would look like if partial effort still added up.
Where this practice comes from
Adapted from Day 9 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
